title: Elaphoglossum vagans
intro: Elaphoglossum vagans is a species of fern, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, protect against cell damage, and support overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Elaphoglossum vagans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support respiratory health and relieve symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum vagans is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Elaphoglossum, which is a group of epiphytic ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with over 2,000 species worldwide. Elaphoglossum vagans is typically classified as a member of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. This classification places Elaphoglossum vagans within the kingdom Plantae, along with all other plants.
